concerns.text: The plan cites `https://huggingface.co/datasets/7eu7d7/HCP-Diffusion-datas/resolve/main/Lexica.art.parquet` as the source for HCP resting-state fMRI time-series. The filename 'Lexica.art' strongly indicates this is an image generation dataset, not neuroimaging data. Relying on this URL for fMRI time-series will cause the pipeline to fail or process incorrect data. The plan must identify a verified source containing actual fMRI NIfTI files or pre-extracted time-series matrices, not just behavioral metadata.

The plan assumes verified URLs contain 'pre-extracted' time-series or raw NIfTI files to fit within free-tier CI limits. However, the listed URLs (e.g., `train.csv`, `LC.zip`) appear to be behavioral metadata or unrelated archives. If these files do not contain the 200-parcel time-series required for the Louvain sliding-window analysis, the 'synthetic data' fallback described in the Decision Log becomes the *only* path, rendering the 'HCP data' citation misleading for the primary analysis. The plan must explicitly distinguish between the source of behavioral scores (DSST) and the source of fMRI data, ensuring the latter is actually available or clearly marked as simulated.
